saints.name: Martyrs Pamphilius the Presbyter, Valens the Deacon, and those with them, at Caesarea in Palestine

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, Pamphilius and his companions, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: Faced with terrible sufferings, / the fearless athletes of the Lord rejoiced; / they conversed with one another ignoring the flesh. / Therefore, they have inherited eternal glory, / ever praying for us who praise their struggles.

saints.name: Saint Maruthas, Bishop of Martyropolis in Mesopotamia

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Maruthas, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

saints.name: Persian Martyrs in Martyropolis in Mesopotamia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Flavian, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
Your Martyr Elias, O Lord,  / by his contest has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God;  / for, possessed of Your strength,  / he laid the tyrants low and shattered the powerless boldness of the demons.  / By his supplications, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You shone forth in your splendor as a bright star,  / proclaiming Christ to this temporal world, O Martyr Elias, /  vanquishing the snares of false gods,  / you revealed the true light to the faithful,  / and you always intercede on behalf of us all.
